checks.finding: Core round-2 ambiguities (effort-resolution priority, welded carve-out) are now fully resolved with an explicit priority table (requirements.md:177-212, 208-212) and dedicated AC-053. Residual Minor items only: (1) T-007's Main Workflow entry (requirements.md:819-825) does not state whether its new tests/effort-policy-flip suite (AC-041) is registered in run-all.sh/test.yml, though the Assumptions bullet's explicit four-task list (T-001/003/005/006) implicitly excludes T-007 and acceptance-tests.md's Notes frame TEST-041..046 as release-gated rather than CI-lane; (2) AC-054(iii)'s 'value outside the model's own supported_efforts vocabulary' phrase superficially overlaps AC-009's per-model clamp condition, resolved on careful read by risk_effort_matrix's model-agnostic schema but could reuse AC-052's explicit {low,medium,high,xhigh} phrasing for precision. Neither rises to a concrete two-implementer divergence.

The round-2 Critical contradiction (test.yml direct-edit plan vs. confirmed R-10 protection) is fully resolved and internally consistent across Assumptions bullet 4, Roles and Permissions, Main Workflows T-001/T-003/T-005/T-006, Edge Cases, Risks, and the rewritten 3-part AC-027/TEST-027 (requirements.md:562-578,745-767,771-825,859-867,918-939). Verified no new conflict introduced by the --requested-effort welded carve-out (AC-010 vs AC-053 are explicitly kept disjoint from AC-007's golden scope) or by AC-054's malformed-registry rejection against AC-009's clamp behavior (different validation layers: static registry-structure vs. dynamic post-selection clamp).

Negative/boundary/failure coverage is comprehensive: AC-004/AC-007 mutation-based negative canaries, AC-009 clamp+xhigh-gate, AC-013 v1/v2 candidates-file divergence, AC-019/AC-020 write-boundary positive/read-boundary pair, AC-024 bidirectional field-population lock, AC-025 backward-compat, AC-051 host-independent degradation generalization, AC-052 CLI-injection rejection (whitespace/flag/separator shapes), AC-053 welded-override carve-out, and new AC-054/TEST-054's 3-category malformed-registry rejection (empty/non-array supported_efforts, out-of-enum effort_control, malformed risk_effort_matrix) with a MODEL_SELECTION_ERROR diagnostic mirroring v1's existing rejection posture.

All 5 Assumptions bullets (requirements.md:895-939) are grounded in file:line investigation citations (select-agent-model.sh:155-184, sdd-hook-guard.py:886-927, guard_invariants.py:4) or explicit git-verified facts (A3 ancestor check) with stated re-verification discipline at task-implementation time. Open Questions: OQ-001/OQ-002/OQ-004 RESOLVED; OQ-003 explicitly PARTIALLY RESOLVED with the automation gap correctly mirrored in Non-goals ('Building an automated CI gate...is explicitly out of scope') rather than left as a silent unresolved risk.

Human-approval boundaries are in scope and testable: the 4 protected-reviewer-file + test.yml human-copy/cp procedure is backed by AC-019/AC-020/AC-027 (write-boundary proof pairs plus the 3-part test.yml check); T-007's prerequisite gate and separate-release requirement are backed by AC-045/AC-046; the exclusive scripts/bump-version.sh release channel is backed by AC-050. No untestable or missing governance boundary found.

Spec is exceptionally detailed (explicit field-definition scoping, priority-order table, security boundaries, risk/mitigation pairs) and does not require a design reviewer to invent product behavior. Residual Minor gap: AC-054/TEST-054's owning task among T-001/T-002/T-005 is left implicit (its test target is stated as 'and/or' between two suite files, and no Main Workflow task enumerates it) -- this is a task-ordering/ownership nicety explicitly protected from failing under the calibration's False-Positive Guard ('do not fail...for omitting implementation tasks or task ordering'), not a missing product-behavior definition.
